events.title: Israel intensifies strikes on Lebanon, killing 22
events.desc: Lebanon's health ministry reported that 22 people, including eight children, were killed in intensified Israeli airstrikes on Wednesday. The attacks targeted approximately 40 locations in southern and eastern Lebanon, occurring on the eve of new peace talks between Lebanon and Israel in Washington. Hezbollah remains opposed to these negotiations.

events.title: Kyiv building collapses after Russian strike
events.desc: A building in the Ukrainian capital, Kyiv, has collapsed following a Russian strike involving drones and missiles. The incident damaged several buildings, with reports indicating residents may be trapped within the debris.

events.title: South Korean official links Iran to Hormuz ship attack
events.desc: A senior South Korean official suggested that the likelihood of an entity other than Iran being responsible for the attack on a South Korean cargo vessel near the Strait of Hormuz is low. South Korea is analyzing intelligence shared by the U.S. regarding the May 4 attack, which caused a fire and damaged the vessel.

events.title: US hopes China will press Iran to change course in Gulf
events.desc: The United States is seeking to persuade China to take a more active role in encouraging Iran to de-escalate its actions in the Gulf. U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio expressed this hope in an interview, highlighting the ongoing tensions and the need for diplomatic pressure on Iran.

events.title: Lebanon and Israel to hold new peace talks in Washington
events.desc: Lebanon and Israel are scheduled to commence new peace talks in Washington, D.C. This comes as their latest ceasefire is nearing its end. The negotiations are being brokered by the United States, following an earlier ceasefire extension announced in April.

events.title: Hezbollah support endures in south Lebanon amid ongoing conflict
events.desc: Despite ongoing Israeli attacks and occupation, many residents in southern Lebanon continue to support Hezbollah, viewing the armed group as the primary force capable of defending them. This support persists even as a ceasefire has not halted the conflict with Israel.

events.title: Israeli strikes in southern Lebanon kill 22, including 8 children
events.desc: The Lebanese health ministry has reported that 22 individuals, eight of whom were children, were killed in a series of Israeli airstrikes in southern Lebanon. The attacks reportedly hit approximately 40 locations, intensifying amidst ongoing tensions.

events.title: Efforts to destroy Iran's police infrastructure tracked
events.desc: Bellingcat has identified at least 80 police stations and related infrastructure belonging to law enforcement agencies and the Basij paramilitary group that have been damaged or destroyed since the start of the U.S. and Israeli war against Iran. Experts suggest the aim is to degrade the Iranian regime's repressive capacity.

events.title: Pakistan's strategy of limited war may not keep conflict contained
events.desc: A previous Indian missile strike on Pakistani air bases saw Islamabad deliberately withhold long-range missile use, choosing not to escalate. However, nearly a year later, the sustained air conflict raises questions about whether Pakistan's logic of limited war can truly keep conflicts contained.

events.title: South Korean vessel attacked in Strait of Hormuz
events.desc: A South Korean vessel was attacked in the Strait of Hormuz on May 4, 2026. President Donald Trump urged Seoul to join a U.S.-led operation to secure the waterway, a proposal South Korea is reviewing. This event highlights the ongoing maritime crisis in the region.

events.title: Iran war live: Tehran slams 'collusion' as Netanyahu 'secretly' visits UAE
events.desc: Iran's first vice president stated that Tehran's claim to the Strait of Hormuz is 'established and the matter is closed.' This comes as reports emerge of an alleged secret visit by Israeli Prime Minister Netanyahu to the UAE, which the UAE has denied.

events.title: Israel passes law allowing death penalty for 7 October attackers
events.desc: Israel has enacted a law that permits the death penalty and public trials for individuals involved in the Hamas-led attacks of October 7. This legislative change comes amidst ongoing tensions and legal proceedings related to the attacks.

events.title: Iran demands guarantees for World Cup participation
events.desc: The Iranian Football Federation (FFIRI) has submitted 10 conditions to FIFA concerning its participation in the upcoming World Cup. These demands are presented amid the ongoing conflict and broader geopolitical tensions involving Iran.

events.title: UK promises naval assets for Strait of Hormuz defence
events.desc: The United Kingdom has pledged to deploy jets, drones, and a warship to support the mission defending the Strait of Hormuz. Over 40 nations are involved in this initiative, which is set to commence once conditions permit.

events.title: Internet access partially restored in Iran after months of blackout
events.desc: Following months of a near-total internet blackout during the Middle East war, limited internet access has been restored in Iran through a privileged service. This partial restoration has drawn public criticism, as millions remain unable to connect since the U.S. and Israeli attacks in February.
